name: Saying “hi” to Microsoft’s Phi-4-reasoning
abstract: This article discusses Microsoft's release of the new Phi-4 reasoning sub-family of small language models (Phi-4-reasoning, Phi-4-reasoning-plus, and Phi-4-mini-reasoning) under the MIT license. It highlights their availability via Ollama and a peculiar tendency to engage in extensive "overthinking" or detailed chain-of-thought processes, even for simple greetings, as dictated by their system prompt. The author compares this behavior to Qwen 3, which exhibits less verbose internal reasoning. While questioning the general applicability of such verbose reasoning models for everyday tasks, the article acknowledges their potential benefits for complex coding, debugging, and mathematical problems. It also notes a similar "overthinking" behavior in NVIDIA's OpenCodeReasoning-Nemotron-32B model.
